auditScope: First-time audit (file previously absent). Scanned signup/login docs, password-reset/recovery docs, theme/branding docs, and all files for veloso.dev references, per Step 0 instructions for a first-time audit. Did not re-derive prior ADRs (domain-generic policy, MFA passkey-only scope) which were treated as authoritative per user-supplied context.
architectureNote: This repo is a Docker Compose stack (authentik + PostgreSQL + Redis, see docker-compose.yml) wrapping goauthentik/authentik, a third-party prebuilt identity-provider image. There is no bespoke frontend source (no Next.js/React/HTML) in this repo for login, signup, password reset, or theming — authentik ships its own compiled web UI (Django-templated 'flow executor'). Branding is done via authentik's own 'Brand' object (logo/favicon/custom CSS) through its admin UI/API, and optionally by mounting custom Django templates into the /templates volume already wired in docker-compose.yml (${SSO_BASE_PATH}/authentik/custom-templates:/templates). Because of this, the product-frontend-scaffolder's UI-scaffolding actions (writing signup/login page components, a theme-toggle component, etc.) do not apply in the literal sense — there is no git-tracked frontend source to scaffold them into. The underlying invariants (password reset, passkey login, theme control, signature) are tracked below as configuration/documentation tasks against authentik instead, and the project is NOT recorded as a blanket non-applicable exception — each invariant is assessed on its own merits.

parentUmbrellaFrontend.notes: No separate umbrella product frontend exists or is implied by this repo. authentik's own web UI (login/flow executor + admin console) is the entire user-facing surface this project controls. There is no multi-product portal to scaffold here; authentik's built-in 'My Applications' launch page already serves that role once apps are registered. Nothing to scaffold in this repo.

signupPage.notes: No self-service signup flow exists, and none is planned. Per docs/first-sso-configuration.md, users are admin-created (admin sets an initial password or triggers authentik's recovery-link flow so the user sets their own). This is the correct posture for an internal SSO gateway that admin-controls access to NAS/self-hosted apps — open self-service signup would be a security regression, not a missing feature. Not tracked as a gap.

loginPage.notes: authentik's built-in login flow provides username/password. Passkey (WebAuthn) as a second factor is documented as active, in-scope work per ADR-012/docs/security-hardening.md/docs/first-sso-configuration.md §3, but TODO.md marks 'MFA recommendation applied' as unconfirmed/not yet done on the live instance (checkbox unchecked as of the last edit this session found) — per project memory, checkboxes are not to be trusted without live verification, and this sandbox cannot reach the live authentik instance to confirm. Email one-time-code as a forgotten-password fallback is NOT present: authentik has an email-based stage capability but nothing in this repo's docs configures it as a login-time OTP fallback. Rate-limiting of login attempts/code requests is not documented as configured (authentik supports it via flow policies/brute-force stage, but no doc references binding it).

passwordReset.notes: Recovery-flow binding, AUTHENTIK_EMAIL__* SMTP env vars, and the walkthrough (docs/authentik-manual.md, docs/first-sso-configuration.md §8) were added and merged (PR #1). Self-service reset still requires SMTP to actually be configured in .env; until then the admin-triggered recovery-link path is the only working option. Not yet verified against a live end-to-end reset (no SMTP configured as of this update).

themeControl.notes: Implemented as a login-page-only cycling light→dark→system button (matching the zqx convention), deliberately scoped to NOT touch authentik's own native theme mechanism (which still governs the post-login User/Admin interface via a separate, untouched localStorage key/attribute — operator was explicit this must stay out of scope). Delivered via a version-controlled Django template override (authentik-custom-templates/if/flow.html, a pure superset of authentik's own if/flow.html at the pinned tag — confirmed by diff) plus custom CSS, per ADR-014 in docs/decision-log.md and docs/authentik-manual.md 'Login-Page-Only Theme Toggle'. Deployed live 2026-07-22 via SSH (synology-site-deployer credentials): file copied to the live custom-templates mount, sso-authentik-server/worker restarted, both healthy. Confirmed via an actual Chrome browser screenshot (Playwright) that the toggle button renders on the live page. Still needs an actual click-through of all three states and confirmation the post-login interface is unaffected — visually present but not yet exercised.

velosoSignature.notes: Conflict resolved by explicit operator decision (recorded as ADR-013 in docs/decision-log.md): the domain-generic repo policy stays in force for git-tracked source, but the operator overrode it for this one live-deployment element. The veloso.dev binary-dot signature (80-bit ASCII-to-binary encoding, CSS box-shadow dots) is a minimal '·' entry in the live Tenant's footer_links, alongside 'Powered by Systems Not Silos' and a contact-form link — see docs/authentik-manual.md 'Footer Links'. The footer_links entry itself was already live; the CSS that actually renders the dots (and hides the '·' text) was corrected and pushed live 2026-07-22 via ak shell (the old selector was spec-invalid and had no effect). Confirmed via an actual browser screenshot (not just curl): the dot signature renders, and 'Powered by authentik' is visually gone. Known limitation carried forward: authentik's footer_links schema has no aria-hidden equivalent, so a screen reader still announces the entry's name text despite the visual minimization.

logoTitleOverlap.notes: A real browser screenshot taken 2026-07-22 (the first actual visual check of this login page, as opposed to curl/source review) caught a genuine bug: the flow title overlapped the logo, because max-height on the logo's wrapper div doesn't constrain the inside it. Fixed with transform: scale() on the wrapper instead (see ADR-015 in docs/decision-log.md) — two live iterations were needed since the first attempt (scale(0.46) + overflow:hidden) cropped the logo per operator feedback. Final version (scale(0.68), no overflow/cropping) confirmed live via screenshot: full logo visible, no overlap.

favicon.notes: Brand's branding_favicon previously pointed at authentik's own stock icon. Generated a dedicated lightweight multi-resolution .ico (16/32/48px, ~7.9KB) from the existing logo asset rather than reusing the full 1254x1254 ~89KB logo file directly, per ADR-016 in docs/decision-log.md. Uploaded to the NAS media storage (same media/public/ convention as the logo; required sudo since that directory is owned by the container's runtime user, not the login SSH user) and set live via ak shell. Verified: the served page's <link rel="icon"> points at it and returns 200 with the correct image/vnd.microsoft.icon content-type. Neither the source logo nor the generated favicon are committed to this repo, consistent with the existing 'don't commit brand image assets' policy.
